elements.text: 5304.2: Automated income assessment using tax data
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: for self-employed income (Future effective date
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Overview
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligible self-employed income
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Underwriting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Data submission requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Representation and warranty relief eligibility and documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Overview
elements.label: text

elements.text: Asset and income modeler (AIM) automated income assessment using tax data provides
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sellers with the option to use certain Systems (as defined in Section 2401.1(b)) to determine
elements.label: text

elements.text: whether the Seller is eligible for relief from enforcement of certain representations and
elements.label: text

elements.text: warranties related to the Borrower’s self-employed income.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must obtain the Borrower’s tax data and submit all required data and information
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the applicable System. Based on the data submitted, the System will assess for
elements.label: text

elements.text: representation and warranty relief eligibility and return the results of the assessment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The method to calculate income assessed by the applicable System may differ from income
elements.label: text

elements.text: calculated using the methods described in Section 5304.1.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Representation and warranty relief eligibility is contingent on the Seller’s compliance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the requirements of this section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: References in this section to “service provider” mean a third-party service provider
elements.label: text

elements.text: designated by Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Tax returns uploaded to a service provider
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller obtains federal income tax returns from the Borrower, the Seller will upload
elements.label: text

elements.text: the tax returns to a service provider, which will produce a report (“Income Calculation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Report”) consisting of data extracted from the Borrower’s tax returns.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller may also request from the Internal Revenue Service (IRS), via the service
elements.label: text

elements.text: provider, a tax transcript of the Borrower’s federal individual income tax return(s). If
elements.label: text

elements.text: both tax returns and tax transcripts are obtained, Loan Product Advisor® will compare
elements.label: text

elements.text: data from the Borrower’s tax return to data from the tax transcript for assessment of tax
elements.label: text

elements.text: return data representation and warranty relief eligibility and return the results of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: assessment on the Feedback Certificate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Tax transcripts obtained from a service provider
elements.label: text

elements.text: Alternatively, the Seller may request from the IRS, via a service provider, a tax transcript
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Borrower’s federal individual income tax return(s). Loan Product Advisor will
elements.label: text

elements.text: retrieve the tax transcript data from the service provider and assess for income and data
elements.label: text

elements.text: representation and warranty relief eligibility and return the results of the assessment on
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Feedback Certificate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Freddie Mac Income Calculator using tax return or tax transcript data
elements.label: text

elements.text: When data from the Borrower’s federal individual income tax returns or tax transcripts is
elements.label: text

elements.text: submitted into the Freddie Mac Income Calculator, a Freddie Mac Income Calculator
elements.label: text

elements.text: Certificate is produced that details the results of the Freddie Mac Income Calculator
elements.label: text

elements.text: submission, including the calculated income and representation and warranty relief
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligibility for income and data, if applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Eligible self-employed income
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Tax return
elements.label: text

elements.text: Self-employed income is an eligible income type for an automated assessment using tax
elements.label: text

elements.text: return data if it is derived from the following business structures:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Sole proprietorships reported on IRS Schedule C
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ S corporations reported on IRS Form 1120S (including compensation of officers
elements.label: text

elements.text: reported on IRS Form W-2), IRS Form 8825 and IRS Schedule K-1 (Form 1120S)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Partnerships reported on IRS Form 1065, IRS Form 8825 and IRS Schedule K-1
elements.label: text

elements.text: (Form 1065)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Tax transcript
elements.label: text

elements.text: Self-employed income is an eligible income type for an automated assessment using tax
elements.label: text

elements.text: transcript data when it is derived from a sole proprietorship and reported on IRS Schedule
elements.label: text

elements.text: C.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: If other self-employed income is reflected on other tax schedules (e.g., Schedule E
elements.label: text

elements.text: or Schedule F), the Mortgage is not eligible for an automated assessment using tax
elements.label: text

elements.text: transcripts.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Underwriting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) General underwriting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Borrower must have been self-employed and received income from the same eligible
elements.label: text

elements.text: income source (i.e., business) on the Income Calculation Report, the Freddie Mac Income
elements.label: text

elements.text: Calculator Certificate or Form 91, Income Calculations (or a similar alternative form as
elements.label: text

elements.text: described in Section 5304.1) for the most recent two years.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower’s business has been in existence for less than five years or the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: has owned the business for less than five years, the Borrower’s federal income tax returns
elements.label: text

elements.text: or tax transcripts must reflect at least two years of receipt of income and/or losses from
elements.label: text

elements.text: the business. Income reported on the business tax returns but not on personal tax returns
elements.label: text

elements.text: must not be considered as stable monthly income.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must have no knowledge, information or documentation that contradicts an
elements.label: text

elements.text: expectation that the income will continue for at least the next three years.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Federal income tax return and tax transcript requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The tax returns or tax transcripts used in the automated income assessment must be the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s individual and business, if applicable, U.S. federal income tax return(s) that
elements.label: text

elements.text: were most recently filed with the IRS. Sellers are encouraged to always confirm with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower that the tax return(s) or tax transcripts provided are the most recent filed with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the IRS.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The individual tax return year most recently filed with the IRS must correspond to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: business tax return year most recently filed with the IRS for all businesses (e.g., when the
elements.label: text

elements.text: most recently filed individual tax return year is 2024, the most recently filed business tax
elements.label: text

elements.text: return year must also be 2024).
elements.label: text

elements.text: The income and/or loss reflected in the federal income tax returns or tax transcripts must
elements.label: text

elements.text: be reported for the same business on the same tax form structure (e.g., the business
elements.label: text

elements.text: cannot be reported as a sole proprietorship and then converted to a partnership).
elements.label: text

elements.text: The tax returns must not be any of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Filed on a fiscal year basis
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Handwritten
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An amended return
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ From a U.S. Territory
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower has amended their tax returns, then tax transcripts are not acceptable for
elements.label: text

elements.text: assessment through the applicable System.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If using tax transcript(s), the assessment requires the most recent calendar year; a tax
elements.label: text

elements.text: extension is not allowed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Inconsistent or contradictory information
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller is responsible for investigating and resolving any inconsistent or contradictory
elements.label: text

elements.text: information contained in the Income Calculation Report, the Freddie Mac Income
elements.label: text

elements.text: Calculator Certificate, Form 91 (or a similar alternative form), federal income tax returns,
elements.label: text

elements.text: tax transcripts and/or the Mortgage file. For example:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Seller determines that a business from which positive Borrower income is
elements.label: text

elements.text: calculated is no longer in operation, the Seller must exclude it from the qualifying
elements.label: text

elements.text: income, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Mortgage receives a tax return data representation and warranty relief result of
elements.label: text

elements.text: “Not Eligible,” as described in Section 5304.2(e)(iii) below, due to inconsistencies
elements.label: text

elements.text: between the tax transcript returned by the IRS via the service provider and the tax
elements.label: text

elements.text: return(s) uploaded to the service provider, the Seller is required to resolve the
elements.label: text

elements.text: discrepancy
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller finds discrepancies, it must update the information in the applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 5305: Other Income
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 5305.1: Requirements for all other income (non-employment/non-self￾employment) (02/04/26)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Requirements applicable to all other income types
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Alimony, child support or separate maintenance payments requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Capital gains requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Dividend and interest requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Foster-care income requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Housing Choice Voucher (HCV) homeownership program (also referred to as Section 8) requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Housing or parsonage allowance requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Long-term disability income requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgage Credit Certificates requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Notes receivable requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Public assistance income (e.g., Temporary Assistance for Needy Families (TANF)) requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Retirement account distributions as income requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Retirement income requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Royalty payments requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Social Security Supplemental Security Income (SSI) requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Survivor and dependent benefit income requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Trust income requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Tax-exempt income requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Additional requirements for income from a foreign source (i.e., non-employment/non-self-employment income)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Requirements applicable to all other income types
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must evaluate the stability, consistency and continued receipt of all other income (i.e., non-employment/non-self￾employment income) used for qualifying, including income types not specifically addressed in this section. The Seller must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Determine whether documentable continuance is applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Consider factors that include, but are not limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Whether the payments are received pursuant to a written agreement, court decree, government program, law and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: regulation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The length of time the payments have been received
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The regularity of receipt of the income
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The consistency of the amount of income
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The availability of procedures to compel payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Whether full or partial payments have been made
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The age of each child for which support and/or benefit payments are made (if applicable)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Applicable eligibility criteria governing the continued receipt of the income
elements.label: text

elements.text: For general requirements with respect to income history, stability and continuance, refer to Section 5301.1.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Alimony, child support or separate maintenance payments requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following table contains the requirements for income from alimony, child support and separate maintenance payments:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Alimony, child support or separate maintenance payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: Topic Stable monthly income requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Minimum history of
elements.label: text

elements.text: receipt
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payments in the full amount must have been consistently received for the most recent six months.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The income must not be considered for qualifying if any of the following apply:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The payor has been obligated to make payments for less than six months
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The payments are not for the full amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The payments are not received on a consistent basis
elements.label: text

elements.text: Continuance Document the payor is obligated to make payments to the Borrower for at least three years from
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Application Received Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Alimony, child support or separate maintenance payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: Topic Stable monthly income requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documentation ■ Documentation of the payor’s obligation(s) for the previous six months, including the amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the duration of the obligation, with one or more of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Signed court order
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Final divorce decree
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Legally binding separation agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Legally binding child support agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Other legally binding documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For child support income, evidence of the ages of the children for which child support is
elements.label: text

elements.text: received
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Receipt of the alimony, child support and/or separate maintenance payments for the most
elements.label: text

elements.text: recent six months. Documentation of receipt must include one or more of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Evidence that the payments were cashed or deposited into the Borrower’s depository
elements.label: text

elements.text: account at a financial institution

elements.text: subject to penalty (e.g., early withdrawal penalty) may be considered stable monthly qualifying

elements.text: Minimum history of
elements.label: text

elements.text: receipt
elements.label: text

elements.text: The history of receipt necessary to justify a stable monthly qualifying income amount may vary
elements.label: text

elements.text: due to the multiple variables inherent with distributions from retirement accounts. This may
elements.label: text

elements.text: include a range of history from zero to 24 months, depending on the individual circumstances.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Factors the Seller must consider when determining the history of receipt necessary to justify a
elements.label: text

elements.text: stable monthly qualifying income amount include, but are not limited to, the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Frequency and regularity of receipt of the distributions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Length of time the distributions have been taken and whether they establish a stable pattern of
elements.label: text

elements.text: receipt over a given period.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example: Consider whether the distributions are fixed amounts occurring with regular
elements.label: text

elements.text: frequency or are fluctuating amounts occurring with or without regular frequency. For fixed
elements.label: text

elements.text: amounts occurring with regular frequency, a lesser history of receipt may be needed to
elements.label: text

elements.text: determine the amount and stability of the qualifying income than would be needed for
elements.label: text

elements.text: fluctuating amounts. For fluctuating amounts, it may be necessary to obtain a longer history
elements.label: text

elements.text: of receipt to determine the amount and stability of the qualifying income while taking into
elements.label: text

elements.text: consideration whether the overall payments are similar when viewed year over year or with
elements.label: text

elements.text: another similar measure, such as quarter over quarter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The tax rate from the current federal and State income tax withholding tables of the tax￾exempt income (or the tax-exempt portion of the income)

elements.text: Example 1:
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: tax-exempt:

elements.label: text

elements.text: Example 2:
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Borrower’s child support income is $2,000, and the tax rate from the current federal and
elements.label: text

elements.text: State income tax withholding tables is 30%:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ $2,000 + $600 = $2,600 (total qualifying child support income)

elements.text: 5305.2: Automated income assessment with Loan Product Advisor® using
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: account data for other income (06/03/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Overview
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligible income types
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Underwriting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Data submission requirements, representation and warranty relief eligibility and documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Internal Revenue Service (IRS) Form 4506-C requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Overview
elements.label: text

elements.text: Representation and warranty relief eligibility is contingent on the Seller’s compliance with all requirements as set forth in this
elements.label: text

elements.text: section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Asset and income modeler (AIM) automated income assessment using account data, provides Sellers with the option to use Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Product Advisor® to determine whether the Seller is eligible for relief from enforcement of certain representations and warranties
elements.label: text

elements.text: related to the Borrower’s income.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must obtain the Borrower’s account data and submit all required data and information to Loan Product Advisor. Based
elements.label: text

elements.text: on the data submitted, Loan Product Advisor will assess for representation and warranty relief eligibility and return the results of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the assessment on the Feedback Certificate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The method to calculate income assessed by Loan Product Advisor may differ from income calculated using the methods
elements.label: text

elements.text: described in Section 5305.1.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Eligible income types
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following income types, as described in Section 5305.1, are eligible for an automated income assessment using account data:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Alimony, child support or separate maintenance payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Retirement income from social security and pensions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Social Security disability benefits
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Social Security Supplemental Security Income (SSI)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Social Security Survivors benefits
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ VA disability compensation
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Underwriting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) General underwriting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must have no knowledge, information or documentation that contradicts an expectation that the income will
elements.label: text

elements.text: continue for at least the next three years.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must confirm:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Each account on the verification report is owned by at least one Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All assets shown on the verification report are in U.S. dollars located in a U.S. or State-regulated financial institution
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ That the payor(s) is one of the following by using the information in the Mortgage file:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The pension fund(s)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Social Security Administration or VA, and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ For alimony, child support or maintenance payments, that the payments are made either by, or on behalf of, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: responsible party identified in the legally binding documentation (i.e., signed court order, final divorce degree, legally
elements.label: text

elements.text: binding separation agreement, legally binding child support agreement or other legally binding documentation).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: For alimony, child support or separate maintenance payments documentation requirements, see Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5305.2(d)(iii) below.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Age of documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: For the age of the documentation, the expiration date of the verification report reflected on the Last Feedback Certificate
elements.label: text

elements.text: complies with the requirements in Section 5102.4.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Data submission requirements, representation and warranty relief eligibility and documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Data submission requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages with an automated income assessment using Loan Product Advisor, the Seller must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Submit the income amount used to underwrite the Mortgage to Loan Product Advisor
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Investigate and resolve any inconsistent or contradictory information between the verification report, information contained
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Form 65, Uniform Residential Loan Application (including the Borrower’s income and employment representations),
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the Mortgage file and, if applicable, resubmit the correct information to Loan Product Advisor
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If after the initial submission, the Seller obtains an updated verification report, the Seller must resubmit the account data to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Product Advisor
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Representation and warranty relief results on Feedback Certificate
elements.label: text

elements.text: If multiple Loan Product Advisor submissions are made, Seller’s eligibility for representation and warranty relief will be based
elements.label: text

elements.text: on the results on the Last Feedback Certificate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When income representation and warranty eligibility results are provided on the Last Feedback Certificate, the representation
elements.label: text

elements.text: and warranty relief available is described in the following table:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Income representation and warranty relief based on Feedback Certificate result
elements.label: text

elements.text: Eligibility result Representation and warranty relief
elements.label: text

elements.text: Eligible The Seller is relieved from enforcement of the following representations and warranties:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Income representation and warranty relief based on Feedback Certificate result
elements.label: text

elements.text: Eligibility result Representation and warranty relief
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Accuracy of the income calculation related to eligible income types on the verification
elements.label: text

elements.text: report, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Accuracy and integrity of the data on the verification report
elements.label: text

elements.text: Partial When partial income representation and warranty relief is granted for a source(s) as
elements.label: text

elements.text: confirmed by the feedback message, the Seller is relieved from enforcement of the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: representations and warranties:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Accuracy of the income calculation related to eligible income types on the verification
elements.label: text

elements.text: report, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Accuracy and integrity of the data on the verification report
elements.label: text

elements.text: Not eligible The Seller is not eligible for relief from enforcement of representations and warranties
elements.label: text

elements.text: related to the Borrower’s income.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unavailable The Seller is not eligible for relief from enforcement of representations and warranties
elements.label: text

elements.text: related to the Borrower’s income.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Documentation requirements based on representation and warranty relief result on Feedback Certificate
elements.label: text

elements.text: When income representation and warranty eligibility results are provided on the Last Feedback Certificate, the Seller must
elements.label: text

elements.text: maintain in the Mortgage file the income documentation indicated on the Last Feedback Certificate in lieu of other
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation required in Section 5305.1.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additional documentation requirements are described in the following table:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documentation requirements based on representation and warranty relief result on the Feedback Certificate
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: When alimony, child support or separate maintenance payment income is used, the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: must maintain in the Mortgage file:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A copy of the legally binding documentation verifying the payor’s obligation for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: previous six months, including the amount and the duration of the obligation, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For child support income, evidence of the ages of the children for which child support is
elements.label: text

elements.text: received
elements.label: text

elements.text: When there are multiple income sources and one or more is eligible for income
elements.label: text

elements.text: representation and warranty relief, for the income source(s) not eligible for representation
elements.label: text

elements.text: and warranty relief, the Seller must either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Verify and document the income as required by the Guide, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Remove the income and resubmit the Mortgage to Loan Product Advisor
elements.label: text

elements.text: Partial The Seller must maintain the verification report in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When alimony, child support or separate maintenance payment income is used, the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: must maintain in the Mortgage file:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A copy of the legally binding documentation verifying the payor’s obligation for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: previous six months, including the amount and the duration of the obligation, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For child support income, evidence of the ages of the children for which child support is
elements.label: text

elements.text: received
elements.label: text

elements.text: When there are multiple income source(s) and one or more income sources are needed to
elements.label: text

elements.text: qualify the Borrower, document the income source(s) as required by the feedback messages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Not eligible The income must be verified and documented as required by the Guide.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documentation requirements based on representation and warranty relief result on the Feedback Certificate
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) IRS Form 4506-C requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages that receive an income representation and warranty result of either “Eligible” or “Partial” with a feedback message
elements.label: text

elements.text: indicating that no further documentation is required for the income reflected on the verification report, if all of the Borrower’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: income is from an eligible income type described above in Section 5305.2(b), the Seller does not need to obtain the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A signed IRS Form 4506-C (or alternate form acceptable to the IRS that authorizes the release of comparable tax information
elements.label: text

elements.text: to a third party), or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A signed Commonwealth of Puerto Rico Form 2907 or Form 4506-C (or an alternate form that authorizes the release of
elements.label: text

elements.text: comparable tax information to a third party), for income that is derived from sources in Puerto Rico, Guam or the U.S. Virgin
elements.label: text

elements.text: Islands and is exempt from federal income taxation under the Internal Revenue Code
elements.label: text

elements.text: For all other Mortgages, the IRS form 4506-C documentation requirements of Section 5302.5 apply.

elements.text: This section contains requirements and guidance for the calculation, documentation, analysis,

elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ General eligibility requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Rental income from subject 1- to 4-unit Investment Property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Rental income from non-subject investment property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Rental income from conversion of a Primary Residence to an Investment Property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Rental income from subject 2- to 4-unit Primary Residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Rental income from non-subject 2- to 4-unit Primary Residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Rental income from an ADU on a subject 1-unit Primary Residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Rental income from a live-in aide residing in a 1-unit Primary Residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Other provisions related to rental income
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) General eligibility requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Stable monthly rental income must be generated from acceptable and verifiable sources and
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be reasonably expected to continue for at least the next three years. For each income
elements.label: text

elements.text: source used to qualify the Borrower, the Seller must determine that both the source and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount of the income are stable. Refer to Section 5301.1 for additional information about
elements.label: text

elements.text: income stability and continuance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Rental income eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rental income generated from the following property and occupancy types may be
elements.label: text

elements.text: considered when determining the stable monthly income:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ 1-unit Primary Residence:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Rental income from a live-in aide, regardless of the type of housing provided, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Rental income from an ADU
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ 2- to 4-unit Primary Residence (rental income is eligible from units that are not
elements.label: text

elements.text: occupied by the Borrower)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Subject 1- to 4-unit Investment Property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Non-subject investment property owned by the Borrower (not restricted to residential
elements.label: text

elements.text: property (e.g., commercial permitted))
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rental income generated from the Borrower’s second home may not be used as stable
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly income.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Rental income generated from ADUs
elements.label: text

elements.text: When determining stable monthly income, rental income generated from an ADU may be
elements.label: text

elements.text: considered for:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Subject 1-unit Primary Residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Subject 1-unit Investment Property
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition, rental income generated from one or more ADUs on a non-subject
elements.label: text

elements.text: investment property may be considered when determining stable monthly income.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 5306.1(g) below for requirements related to rental income
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligibility for a 1-unit Primary Residence with an ADU. Refer to Section 4501.6(b) for
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements related to rental income eligibility for a 1-unit Primary Residence with an
elements.label: text

elements.text: ADU for Home Possible® Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 5601.2 for the property eligibility and appraisal requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: related to the subject property with an ADU.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rental income generated from a subject 1- to 4-unit Investment Property is eligible for
elements.label: text
